ALL SPECIALTIES / PHYSICAL THERAPIST / NEW HAMPSHIRE

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

New Hampshire Physical Therapist License

New Hampshire Physical Therapist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Physical Therapist applications in New Hampshire run through New Hampshire OPLC — Physical Therapy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

  1. STEP 1

    Create an OPLC Online Licensing Portal Account

    Visit the OPLC Online Licensing Portal and complete the registration process for a new user account if you do not already have one.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Start a New Physical Therapist Application

    Log into your OPLC Online Licensing Portal account, navigate to the 'Apply for a New License' section, and select the Physical Therapist application.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Complete Online Application and Pay Fee

    Fill out all required sections of the online application form, upload necessary initial documents, and pay the $100 application fee through the portal.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Submit Required Supporting Documentation

    Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program, NPTE scores, and verifications of licensure from any other states where you hold or have held a license, to be sent directly to the New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Await Board Review and Approval

    The New Hampshire Physical Therapy Governing Board will review your complete application and all supporting documents. You can monitor the status of your application via your OPLC Online Licensing Portal account until approval and license issuance.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$100

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
New Hampshire OPLC — Physical Therapy
Phone
(603) 271-2152
Website
oplc.nh.gov/physical-therapy
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Physical Therapist renewal in New Hampshire follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$100
Continuing education
24 contact hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a New Hampshire physical therapist license cost?

For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for physical therapists?

Yes. New Hampshire participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Hampshire without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to New Hampshire?

Current board processing time is 2 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$100

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years · $100

Licensing board

New Hampshire OPLC — Physical Therapy

oplc.nh.gov/physical-therapy

(603) 271-2152

This page is maintained as a reference for physical therapists and recruiters evaluating New Hampshire assignments.
